title: Estimate Sums and Differences
essential_question: How can you estimate sums and differences of whole numbers?

pretest.question: Estimate 342 + 185 by rounding to the nearest hundred.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. 342→300, 185→200, sum=500.

pretest.question: Estimate 5,678 + 3,219 by rounding to the nearest thousand.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. 5,678→6,000, 3,219→3,000, sum=9,000.

pretest.question: Estimate 612 − 288 by rounding to the nearest hundred.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. 612→600, 288→300, diff=300.

pretest.question: 547 rounds to 500 when rounded to the nearest hundred.

feedback.incorrect: The answer is True. Tens digit 4 < 5, round down.

pretest.question: Estimate 23,456 + 11,234 by rounding to the nearest ten-thousand.

learn.title: Why Estimate?
learn.content: Estimation helps you check if an answer is reasonable. When you round numbers before adding or subtracting, the sum or difference is close to the real answer but easier to find.
learn.tts: Estimation helps you check if an answer is reasonable. When you round numbers before adding or subtracting, the sum or difference is close to the real answer but easier to find.

learn.title: Rounding Review
learn.content: To round to the nearest hundred, look at the tens digit. If it is 5 or more, round up. If it is less than 5, round down. Example: 342 → tens digit is 4, so round down to 300.
learn.tts: To round to the nearest hundred, look at the tens digit. If it is 5 or more, round up. If it is less than 5, round down. Example: 342 → tens digit is 4, so round down to 300.

learn.title: Estimate a Sum
learn.content: Estimate 473 + 219 to the nearest hundred. Round each number: 473→500, 219→200. Then add: 500 + 200 = 700. The estimate is 700.
learn.tts: Estimate 473 + 219 to the nearest hundred. Round each number: 473→500, 219→200. Then add: 500 + 200 = 700. The estimate is 700.

learn.title: Estimate a Difference
learn.content: Estimate 832 − 267 to the nearest hundred. Round: 832→800, 267→300. Subtract: 800 − 300 = 500.
learn.tts: Estimate 832 − 267 to the nearest hundred. Round: 832→800, 267→300. Subtract: 800 − 300 = 500.

learn.title: Round to Thousands
learn.content: For larger numbers, round to the nearest thousand. Example: 5,842 → look at the hundreds digit (8). 8 ≥ 5, round up to 6,000.
learn.tts: For larger numbers, round to the nearest thousand. Example: 5,842 → look at the hundreds digit (8). 8 ≥ 5, round up to 6,000.

learn.title: Choose the Place
learn.content: Choose a rounding place that makes sense: hundreds for 3-digit numbers, thousands for 4-digit, ten-thousands for 5-digit. A closer estimate comes from rounding to a smaller place.
learn.tts: Choose a rounding place that makes sense: hundreds for 3-digit numbers, thousands for 4-digit, ten-thousands for 5-digit. A closer estimate comes from rounding to a smaller place.

try.question: Estimate 458 + 321 by rounding to the nearest hundred.
hints: Think about what estimate sum difference means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.
solution_steps: Round 458 to the nearest hundred: tens digit 5, round up to 500.
Round 321 to the nearest hundred: tens digit 2, round down to 300.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 800. Work through each step carefully.
